title: How to Register for JAMB UTME: A Step-by-Step Guide for First-Timers
author: Tklesson Team
content:
Registering for the JAMB UTME is the first official step toward university admission in Nigeria. The process involves creating a profile, obtaining a National Identification Number, selecting your subjects and institutions, and completing biometric verification. Mistakes made during registration can delay your admission or disqualify you entirely. Here is a step-by-step guide to navigating the UTME registration process correctly.

Step One: Obtain Your National Identification Number

JAMB requires every candidate to have a National Identification Number, or NIN, before registration. If you do not already have one, you must obtain it from the National Identity Management Commission, or NIMC. This involves visiting a NIMC enrollment center, providing your biometric data including fingerprints and a photograph, and receiving your NIN. The process can take time, and enrollment centers can be crowded, especially during registration season. Start this step early. Do not wait until the JAMB registration deadline is approaching to obtain your NIN. The earlier you get it, the less stressful the rest of the process will be.

Your NIN is linked to your identity. Ensure that the name on your NIN matches the name on your other documents, including your birth certificate and your O'Level results. Discrepancies in your name across documents can cause problems during admission verification. If your name is inconsistent, resolve the inconsistency before you begin JAMB registration.

Step Two: Create Your JAMB Profile

Once you have your NIN, you can create your JAMB profile. This is done by sending an SMS from the phone number you intend to use for all JAMB communications. Send the word NIN followed by a space and your eleven-digit NIN to 55019 or 66019, depending on the current year's instructions. For example, NIN 12345678901. You will receive a confirmation code. Keep this code. You will need it to purchase your e-PIN.

The phone number you use to create your profile is important. It will be linked to your JAMB record for the entire admission process. Use a number that belongs to you and that you will have access to throughout the year. Do not use a parent's number that they might change, or a friend's number that might become unreachable. The number you use is how JAMB will communicate with you about your exam date, your results, and your admission status.

Step Three: Purchase the e-PIN

The e-PIN is the electronic personal identification number that grants you access to the registration portal. It is purchased from approved banks, online payment platforms, or JAMB offices. The cost includes the registration fee and, in some cases, a recommended reading text. The exact amount varies from year to year, so check the current fee schedule on the JAMB website.

When purchasing your e-PIN, you will need to provide your NIN and your profile confirmation code. The e-PIN is unique to you and cannot be transferred. Keep it secure. Anyone with access to your e-PIN and your personal information could potentially register in your name or tamper with your application.

Step Four: Complete Registration at an Accredited CBT Center

JAMB registration must be completed at an accredited Computer-Based Test center. You cannot register from home. Visit an accredited center with your NIN, your e-PIN, and your O'Level result slip or the details of your O'Level results. If you are awaiting results, you can still register, but you must indicate which subjects you are awaiting and upload the results when they become available.

At the CBT center, the registration officer will enter your personal information, your subject combination, and your institution choices into the JAMB portal. Your biometric data, including your fingerprints and photograph, will be captured. This biometric data is used to verify your identity on exam day and during admission. Ensure that the information entered on your behalf is accurate. Read every field before it is submitted. An error in your name, date of birth, subject combination, or institution choices made during registration is difficult and sometimes impossible to correct later.

You will be given a printout of your registration slip. This slip contains your registration number, your subject combination, your chosen institutions, and your exam date and center. Keep this slip safe. You will need it on exam day and throughout the admission process. Make multiple copies. Store a digital copy on your phone or in your email.

Step Five: Select Your Subjects and Institutions

During registration, you will select your four UTME subjects and your preferred institutions. You can choose up to four institutions: first choice, second choice, third choice, and fourth choice. The first choice is typically the most competitive institution you are targeting. The subsequent choices should be progressively less competitive or should include a polytechnic or college of education as a safety option.

Your subject combination must match the requirements for your intended course at your first-choice institution. The JAMB brochure, available at the registration center and online, lists the required subjects for every course at every institution. Consult it before making your selections. A mistake in your subject combination can disqualify you from your intended course regardless of your score.

Choose your institutions realistically. If your JAMB score is likely to be average, do not fill all four choices with highly competitive federal universities. Include a state university, a private university, or a polytechnic among your choices. The goal is to have at least one institution where your likely score is competitive. A candidate who fills all four choices with reach schools and scores below the cut-off for all of them will have nowhere to go. A candidate with a mix of reach and safety schools will have options.

Step Six: Prepare for the Exam

After registration, you will receive information about your exam date, time, and center. Print your exam slip when it becomes available. Visit the exam center ahead of time if possible, or at least plan your route. Arrive early on exam day. Bring your registration slip, your exam slip, and a valid form of identification. Do not bring prohibited items, including phones, watches, or bags. The exam center will have strict rules about what is allowed inside.

Your preparation between registration and the exam is what determines your score. Registration is the administrative step. Preparation is the academic step. Both are necessary. A candidate who registers perfectly but does not prepare will not gain admission. A candidate who prepares extensively but registers incorrectly may be disqualified. Give both steps the attention they deserve.

The Bottom Line

JAMB UTME registration involves obtaining your NIN, creating a profile via SMS, purchasing an e-PIN, completing biometric capture at an accredited CBT center, and selecting your subjects and institutions. Start early, especially with the NIN. Verify every piece of information before it is submitted. Choose your subject combination based on your intended course requirements. Choose your institutions with a mix of competitive and safety options. After registration, shift your focus to exam preparation. The registration gets you a seat. The preparation gets you a score.
